title: The Gombeen-Man (The Rocky Road To Dublin)
content:
I put the sky into my pocket,
        And the sea into my locket,
        And into my breeches-band
        I put the land.

        So I was trotting off to share,
        Among my comrades in the lair,
        Our profits, when a peeler came
        And took my name.

        And now I'm in the County Gaol!
        Will anybody be my bail?
        Will anybody be my bail
        And take me from the County Gaol?
meaning:
James Stephens's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Gombeen-Man (The Rocky Road To Dublin)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
